Thank you for the contribution. Please make sure your PR follows the project's build laws (see LAWS.md) and the contribution guide (CONTRIBUTING.md).
-->

## Summary

<!-- One or two sentences on what this PR changes and why. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds capability)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing behavior to change)
- [ ] Documentation only
- [ ] Refactor / internal cleanup (no user-visible behavior change)
- [ ] Build / tooling / CI

## Related issues

<!-- Link issues this PR closes or relates to. Use "Closes #123" to auto-close on merge. -->

## How was this tested?

<!--
Describe the tests you ran. Include any specific repository fixtures or scan scenarios you exercised. If this affects the engine, list the platforms tested.
-->

## Checklist

- [ ] I have read [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) and [LAWS.md](LAWS.md)
- [ ] My code follows the existing style (lint-clean, type-safe, no `any`)
- [ ] I have added tests that cover my changes
- [ ] All new and existing tests pass locally (`npm test`)
- [ ] Type check passes (`npm run typecheck`)
- [ ] Lint passes (`npm run lint`)
- [ ] I have updated documentation where relevant (README, spec files, or inline doc comments where the *why* is non-obvious)
- [ ] If this affects the PII/HIPAA pipeline, the attestation chain, or Crystal purity, I have explicitly described the impact in the summary above

## Screenshots / output (if UI or artifact change)

<!-- For extension UI changes, attach a screenshot or short clip. For artifact changes, paste a sample. -->

## Additional notes

<!-- Anything else reviewers should know. Trade-offs, follow-ups, known limitations. -->
